question: Does the accepted Q2.6 global replay-tangent intervention reproduce on seeds 1 and 3 under the unchanged gates?

design_lock.permitted_change: authorize seed identity and route outputs into zero4-q26r-v1; the cumulative-tangent intervention, initialization, teachers, corpora, optimizer, budgets, scales, cadence, stop rules, direct authorities, thresholds, and promotion split are inherited byte-for-byte from the diagnostic contract

seed_outcomes.go: the seed has a jointly feasible public checkpoint and its exactly-once disjoint promotion evaluation passes every quantity gate
seed_outcomes.no_go: the run completes validly without the public-and-promotion conjunction before a frozen stop condition
seed_outcomes.execution_failure: the run exits unsuccessfully, times out, duplicates a result, or violates the frozen metric or source envelope

family_rule.decision: go if and only if seeds 1, 2, and 3 each resolve go; otherwise no-go after all three declared results exist
family_rule.promotion_eligible: true if and only if the family decision is go
family_rule.promoted_model_selection: the prospectively selected seed-2 model remains the ZERO.4 candidate; replication seed models are evidence and cannot replace it post hoc
